free_text: RESEARCH REACTOR INSTITUTE, KYOTO
UNIVERSITY
-MEASUREMENT OF NEUTRON TOTAL CROSS SECTION OF
SCANDIUM NEAR 2 KEV-
THE KURRI-LINAC
NEUTRONS PRODUCED UTILIZING THE BREMSSTRAHLUNG
IN A WATER-COOLED TANTALUM TARGET. ELECTRON
ENERGY 30 MEV. BEAM WIDTH 33 NS.
.THE SAMPLES WERE COMPOSED OF SCANDIUM METAL FLAKES,
1 TO 2 MM THICK AND ABOUT 1 CM-SQ IN AREA, PACKED
IN ALUMINUM CANS WITH INNER DIAMETERS OF 5 CM. SAMPLE
THICKNESSES 0.040, 0.265, 0.517, 0.873 IN THE UNIT
ATOMS PER BARN. MAIN IMPURITY 0.175 WEIGHT PER CENT
OF COPPER.
.TRANSMISSION MEASUREMENT FOR TOTAL CROSS SECTIONS.
EXPERIMENTAL RUNNING TIME 15 HR. SAMPLE-IN AND OPEN
RUNS WERE REPEATED SEVERAL TIMES DURING THE
MEASUREMENT TO MINIMIZE THE EFFECTS OF LONG TIME
DRIFTS IN THE EXPERIMENTAL SYSTEM
LI-6 GLASS SCINTILLATOR, 12.7 CM IN DIAMETER
AND 1.27 CM THICK.
.CROSS SECTIONS WERE OBTAINED USING THE LEAST-SQUARE
METHOD ON THE RESULT OF SAMPLE-IN RUNS WITH THE FOUR
DIFFERENT SAMPLES AND A SAMPLE-OUT RUN. IN ALL RUNS
SC AND CO AND A THIN SHEET OF CD WERE PLACED IN THE
NEUTRON BEAM AS NOTCH FILTER AND AN OVERLAP FILTER,
RESPECTIVELY. A BACKGROUND IN EACH SPECTRUM WAS
ESTIMATED BY LINEARLY EXTRAPOLATING BETWEEN DIPS
IN SPECTRA EMANATING FROM RESONANCES IN SCANDIUM
AND COBOLT AT 4.33 KEV AND 132 EV, RESPECTIVELY.
NEUTRONS
.DATA GOT IN PRIVATE COMMUNICATION.
A.L.
.FOR
- BACKGROUND AND DEAD TIME OF THE DETECTOR AND THE
DATA ACQUISITION SYSTEM.
- IMPURITY IN THE TRANSMISSION SAMPLE
- THE TRANSMISSION IN AIR IN SAMPLE-OUT RUNS
A STATISTICAL UNCERTAINTY OBTAINED FROM
THE LEAST-SQUARE METHOD.
.DATA FROM PRIVATE COMMUNICATION.
A.L.
